core_postmortem.headline: SAS owned the cleaner shot-quality profile but lost the possession economy. NYK won outright through offensive rebounding, extra possessions, and enough perimeter shotmaking to overcome SAS efficiency advantages.
core_postmortem.primary_failure_mode_for_SAS_backers: Favorite pricing assumed SAS shot quality and home control would translate into margin. It did not account enough for NYK offensive rebounding and SAS live-ball turnover leakage.
core_postmortem.primary_success_mode_for_NYK: NYK survived a lower shot-quality profile by winning total rebounds 52-45, offensive rebounds 17-7, and creating enough extra possessions to flip a one-point game.

possession_economy.rebounding_edge: NYK +7 total rebounds and +10 offensive rebounds
possession_economy.turnover_edge: NYK committed one fewer turnover and far fewer live-ball turnovers; SAS live-ball TO rate 68.75% versus NYK 46.67%
possession_economy.foul_pressure: SAS drew 23 fouls to NYK's 20, but this did not overcome the rebounding deficit and live-ball turnover damage
possession_economy.net_read: NYK's extra shot volume and second-chance layer neutralized SAS's cleaner shot diet.

NYK.Karl-Anthony Towns: 13 REB, 18 chances, 72.2 REB chance%, 4 box outs with 4 player rebounds on box outs
NYK.Mikal Bridges: 6 REB in 40.9 minutes, 9 chances, strong wing minute load
NYK.Josh Hart: 6 REB in only 18.2 minutes, 11 chances; prior Game 1 15 REB profile remains a rolling-window volatility flag
NYK.Mitchell Robinson: 3 REB, 11 chances, 14.1 minutes; probable/finger status did not remove him from the glass ecosystem

SAS.Victor Wembanyama: 9 REB, 19 chances, 7 contested rebounds, 77.8 contested REB%; high physical burden but not enough possession closure
SAS.Devin Vassell: 9 REB, 12 chances, 75.0 REB chance%; wing rebounding remains sticky
SAS.Dylan Harper: 6 REB, 9 chances, 66.7 chance%; reliable guard rebound support
SAS.Julian Champagnie: 4 REB on 8 chances after 10 REB in Game 1; regression mattered

market_model_lessons.favorite_tax: SAS was priced as a strong home favorite despite NYK's proven possession/rebounding profile. Game 2 confirms caution against overpricing SAS solely from shot quality and star power.
market_model_lessons.total_lesson: The full-game under landed because the market's 216.5-218.5 close left little room for NYK pace control and SAS late-game half-court drag. Final total: 209.
market_model_lessons.team_total_lesson: SAS TT unders were structurally live at 111.5+ because NYK's defensive rebounding was not elite, but NYK's possession pressure and SAS live-ball turnovers disrupted clean volume. NYK TT around 105.5 was correctly tight; final 105 landed near market median.
market_model_lessons.game3_carryover: For Game 3 in New York, apply a NYK possession-economy bump and reduce SAS favorite inflation. Do not let SAS shot-quality superiority alone create an oversized projection gap.

game3_preparation_directives.baseline_adjustment: NYK home-court plus possession economy should narrow the spread baseline materially from Game 2 pricing.
watch_markets_first: NYK spread if market still gives inflated SAS respect
NYK ML if plus-money remains available with model probability above break-even
SAS team total under if market opens 111.5+ again
Full-game total only after pace and lineup confirmation
